title: To amend title III of the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize the program of payments to childrens hospitals that operate graduate medical education programs.
plain_summary: This bill extends a government program that helps pay children's hospitals to train new doctors. The program was set to end in 2023, but this bill would keep it running until 2030. This means children's hospitals will continue to get money from the government to help teach medical students and residents how to become doctors. The program is important because it helps make sure there are enough doctors who know how to take care of children.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1  Program of Payments to Children's Hospitals That Operate Graduate Medical Education Programs
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Public Health Service Act. The law has a program that gives money to children's hospitals that train doctors. Graduate medical education programs are where doctors continue learning after medical school by working in hospitals under supervision. Right now, this program is set to end in two thousand twenty three. This bill would extend the program so it continues until two thousand thirty. The change applies to three different parts of the existing law that all had the same two thousand twenty three end date.
